<style webc:scoped="home-hero">
  :host {
    --offset: -2.3rem;
    padding: 0 2rem;
    color: var(--color-accent-primary);
    margin-bottom: var(--offset);
    transform: translateY(var(--offset));
  }

  .inner {
    display: grid;

    & > * {
      grid-area: 1 / 1;
    }
  }

  .hype-sticker {
    display: block;
    position: relative;
    z-index: 10;
    height: auto;
    rotate: -15deg;
    width: 120px;
    top: 0.5rem;
    left: -0.5rem;
    filter: drop-shadow(0 0 1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2));
    transform: skew(-1deg, 1.5deg);
    z-index: 11;

    &:hover {
      animation: shimmy 0.1s linear alternate 6;
    }
  }

  @keyframes shimmy {
    100% {
      rotate: -10deg;
    }
  }

  .contents {
    position: relative;
    padding-top: 6rem;
    z-index: 10;
  }

  .invocation {
    display: block;
    height: auto;
    padding: 0 2rem;
    width: 100%;
  }

  .intro {
    background: var(--color-bg);
    font-size: var(--font-size-28);
    line-height: 1.8;
    margin-left: auto;
    max-width: var(--g-max-6);
    position: relative;

    p {
      padding: 2.5rem 3.5rem;
    }

    svg {
      position: absolute;

      &:first-of-type {
        top: 1.75rem;
        left: -2.5rem;
        rotate: -8deg;
        transform: scale(0.75);
      }

      &:last-of-type {
        top: 2rem;
        right: -1.5rem;
        rotate: 9deg;
        scale: 0.8;
        transform: scale(0.75);
      }
    }

    strong {
      background-color: var(--color-accent-primary);
      color: var(--color-bg);
      font-family: var(--font-dragonsteel);
      font-size: 145%;
      font-weight: 400;
      line-height: 1.2;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      padding: 0 0.25rem;
    }
  }

  .graphics {
    display: flex;
    overflow: hidden;
    position: relative;
    max-width: 100%;
    mix-blend-mode: color-dodge;
  }

  .graphics:nth-of-type(2),
  .graphics:nth-of-type(3) {
    opacity: 0.4;
  }

  .pane {
    flex: 1;
    overflow: hidden;
    position: relative;

    &::after {
      background: color(display-p3 1 0 0);
      content: "";
      display: block;
      left: 0;
      top: -1px;
      position: absolute;
      height: 100%;
      width: 100%;
      mix-blend-mode: color-dodge;
    }

    img {
      object-fit: cover;
      object-position: center;
      height: 100%;
      width: 100%;
      filter: brightness(2.8);
      transform-origin: center;
    }

    &:nth-child(1) {
      flex-basis: 10%;
      height: 96%;

      img {
        object-position: 10%;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(2) {
      flex-basis: 20%;
      height: 97%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.5rem;

      img {
        object-position: 5%;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(3) {
      flex-basis: 30%;
    }

    &:nth-child(4) {
      flex-basis: 10%;
      height: 98.5%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.75rem;

      img {
        object-position: right;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(5) {
      flex-basis: 12%;
      height: 98.5%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.25rem;

      img {
        object-position: right;
        transform: scaleY(1.2);
      }
    }
  }
</style>

<style>
  html[style*="color-scheme: dark"] .graphics {
    mix-blend-mode: difference;
    filter: contrast(0.5) saturate(3) brightness(0.39);

    .pane img {
      filter: invert() brightness(1.5);
    }
  }
</style>

content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the home-hero component. Let me analyze the responsive issues:

**Structure:**
- Root `div` with `padding: 0 2rem` and a negative offset transform
- `.inner` - a grid where all children stack in `grid-area: 1 / 1`
- `.hype-sticker` - a 120px SVG circle sticker positioned with `top`/`left`
- 3x `.graphics` divs - each contains 5 `.pane` divs with images, displayed as flex
- `.contents` - positioned relative, z-index 10, contains:
  - `.invocation` SVG - full width, padded with `padding: 0 2rem`
  - `.intro` - has `max-width: var(--g-max-6)`, `margin-left: auto`, background, with paragraph and decorative SVGs

Looking at the responsive issues, the `.intro` section's max-width constraint of around 944px likely causes overflow on small screens since there's no minimum width handling to prevent it from extending beyond the viewport.  The `margin-left: auto` pushes content to the right edge, which creates awkward alignment on phones. The 28px font size and 3.5rem horizontal padding are both oversized for mobile, and the decorative SVGs positioned at negative offsets will spill outside the viewport boundaries. The `.invocation` SVG's 1362×711 aspect ratio means it'll take up excessive vertical space on narrow screens.

Looking at the rest of the layout, the `.contents` section has heavy top padding that compounds the spacing issues, and the five-pane flex grid with varying widths will become too cramped to display meaningful content on small devices. The `.hype-sticker` positioned at the top-left corner might need scaling adjustments for very small screens. I notice there aren't any media queries defined yet to handle these responsive issues. responsive breakpoints. The `.intro` text is way too large on mobile at 28px with a 1.8 line-height, and the padding of 2.5rem horizontally consumes too much space on small screens. The `margin-left: auto` pushes everything right when it should be centered or full-width on narrow viewports, and the decorative SVGs positioned at negative offsets will clip or overflow. The `.contents` section has excessive top padding at 6rem for mobile, and the large invocation SVG will scale down to something unusable on a 375px screen.

Beyond that, the `.hype-sticker` at 120px takes up nearly a third of a phone's width, and the narrowest `.graphics` panes with 10% flex-basis become only about 37px wide—too thin to be useful. I'm looking at the project's existing breakpoint patterns (620px, 830px, 1040px, 1240px) to create a responsive strategy that addresses these issues.

content.text:
Now I have a full picture. Here's my analysis:

## Current State

The home-hero component has **zero media queries** — it's entirely static. The structure is:
- A stacking grid (`.inner`) with 3 `.graphics` layers and a `.contents` layer
- `.contents` holds a large SVG (`.invocation`, 1362×711 viewBox) and an `.intro` block with text
- `.intro` is pushed right with `margin-left: auto`, capped at `--g-max-6` (~945px)
- A decorative `.hype-sticker` SVG (120px)

## Issues on Medium/Small Screens

1. **`.intro` padding is too generous** — `padding: 2.5rem 3.5rem` means 56px of horizontal padding. On a 375px phone, that eats ~30% of the content area.

2. **`.intro` font-size stays at 28px** (`--font-size-28`) with `line-height: 1.8` — too large for phones.

3. **`.intro` margin-left: auto** — pushes the block to the right at all sizes. On small screens it should be full-width/centered instead.

4. **Decorative SVGs on `.intro` overflow** — positioned at `left: -2.5rem` and `right: -1.5rem`, they extend outside the container and will clip or cause horizontal scroll on narrow viewports.

5. **`.contents` padding-top: 6rem** — pushes the text content way down on mobile, wasting vertical space.

6. **`.invocation` SVG becomes very short** — at 375px wide (minus padding), the 1362×711 aspect ratio SVG renders at only ~140px tall, making the text art potentially unreadable.

7. **`.hype-sticker` at 120px** — takes up ~32% of a 375px screen width, relatively oversized.

8. **`.graphics` pane widths** — the narrowest panes (10% flex-basis) become ~30-37px wide on phones. May look like thin slivers rather than recognizable image strips.

## Proposed Plan

Using the project's existing breakpoint conventions (`620px`, `~830px`, `1040px`):

### Small screens (default / < 620px)
- Reduce `.intro` padding to ~`1.25rem 1.5rem`
- Reduce `.intro` font-size to `--font-size-20` or similar
- Remove `margin-left: auto` on `.intro` so it's full-width
- Hide or significantly scale down the decorative SVGs on `.intro`
- Reduce `.contents` padding-top to ~`2rem`
- Scale `.hype-sticker` to ~80px
- Reduce `.invocation` SVG horizontal padding
- Consider reducing `.graphics` panes from 5 to 3 (hide the narrowest ones) or letting them all share equal width

### Medium screens (620px – ~1040px)
- Transition padding and font-size to intermediate values
- Decorative SVGs can reappear but scaled/repositioned to avoid overflow
- `.intro` can start using `margin-left: auto` again
- `.hype-sticker` back to ~100px

### Large screens (1040px+)
- Current styles, no changes needed

Want me to go ahead and implement this, or would you like to adjust any part of the plan first?

content.text:
Now I can see the actual problems. Here's what's happening:

## 1440px (Desktop) — Looks good
The hero works as intended. The invocation SVG text is legible, the intro paragraph sits nicely to the right, decorative elements are well-positioned.

## 768px (Tablet) — Starting to break
- The invocation SVG ("in ugly times the real protest is beauty") is getting cramped but still readable
- The intro text block is fine, though the decorative `XX` SVGs are getting close to the edges
- Overall this width is acceptable but on the edge

## 375px (Phone) — Significant issues
1. **Invocation SVG is nearly illegible** — the large hand-lettered text is crushed into a tiny space, especially "protest" and "BEAUTY" are overlapping/barely readable
2. **Intro paragraph text is way too large** — the 28px font size with 1.8 line-height creates a single narrow column of ~3 words per line. It reads like a skinny newspaper column, making every sentence feel fragmented
3. **Intro padding is excessive** — 3.5rem horizontal padding on a 375px screen leaves almost no room for content
4. **Decorative XX SVGs** — positioned at `left: -2.5rem` and `right: -1.5rem`, they're clipped/overlapping with the text at this width
5. **The `<strong>` text** ("20 years of practice, effort, and execution") at 145% of 28px (~41px) is massive on this screen
6. **Hype sticker** at 120px is proportionally large but not terrible

## Plan

Given what I'm actually seeing, here are the real breakpoints where things need to change:

**Small (< ~600px):**
- Reduce `.intro` font-size (maybe `--font-size-18` or `--font-size-20`)
- Reduce `.intro p` padding significantly (`1rem 1.25rem` or similar)
- Scale down or hide the decorative XX SVGs
- Reduce `.contents` padding-top
- The `.invocation` SVG may need its own padding reduced, or the whole contents section could shift so the SVG has more breathing room
- Reduce the `<strong>` font-size percentage

**Medium (~600px – ~900px):**
- Moderate reductions to padding and font-size
- Decorative SVGs repositioned to avoid clipping

Want me to proceed with this, or do you want to adjust anything?

content.thinking:
I see the issue. The `@import` statements at lines 426-442 come AFTER the inline `@layer` blocks. Per the CSS spec, `@import` rules must come before all other rules except `@charset` and `@layer` declarations (the ordering statement on line 1). Having inline `@layer` blocks before `@import` statements makes the `@import` statements invalid — browsers will ignore them.

The `@layer baseline, tokens, components, pages;` declaration on line 1 is fine (it's just a layer order declaration), but the full `@layer baseline { ... }` and `@layer tokens { ... }` blocks are regular rules, and `@import` rules cannot appear after regular rules.
The fix is to move the `@import` statements before those inline layer blocks, since the correct CSS ordering is the layer declaration first, then imports, then the actual layer blocks with content.

content.text:
Found it. The problem is CSS spec ordering: **`@import` rules must come before all other rules** (except `@charset` and bare `@layer` declarations). The inline `@layer baseline { ... }` and `@layer tokens { ... }` blocks are regular rules, so the `@import` statements on lines 426-442 that come after them are being ignored by the browser.

The fix is to move the `@import` statements above the inline layer blocks.
